danger_signals.visual_form: Small dinosaurs popping out of eggs, spiders, saber-tooth tigers, prehistoric monsters
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental threats requiring combat or avoidance; signal presence of hostile territory

danger_signals.visual_form: Objects falling from top of screen
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate danger from above; requires rock shield defensive use

danger_signals.visual_form: Creepy dinosaur graveyard setting with Gothic visual tone
danger_signals.meaning: Final danger zone; boss territory; kidnapper's stronghold location
Environmental danger requiring navigation skill or creature interaction
Environmental hazard zone with altitude and temperature threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Flying lizards, brontosaurus, crocodile in specific contexts
safety_signals.meaning: Passive aid entities; can be consumed (lizards) or ridden (brontosaurus) or used as platforms (crocodile seesaw)
Dual-purpose safety: shields from falling hazards and serve as jumping platforms for bonus access

safety_signals.visual_form: Primeval jungle and cave visual theming
safety_signals.meaning: Early, less hostile world zones before final confrontation; transitional safety relative to graveyard

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Rock-throwing caveman; punk-style mohawk; overweight square-jawed appearance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Guitar band member; domestic context (watching TV before kidnapping); rescue mission protagonist

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Attractive caveman wife; band vocalist; kidnapping victim
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Domestic space (doing laundry when kidnapped); held in dinosaur graveyard; rock band context

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Jealous local bully; pun-named after pop star Gary Glitter
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Creepy dinosaur graveyard hang-out; final boss location; kidnapper identity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Chuck at home watching TV, Ophelia doing laundry in domestic context
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Normal civilian activity; no enemies present; narrative setup state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ophelia knocked unconscious by Garry Gritter; carried to graveyard
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Shift to rescue mission imperative; world becomes hostile territory

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Primeval jungle visuals; small dinosaurs, insects, plants
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Introductory combat and platforming challenges; manageable threat level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Underground cave environment with enclosed spaces
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Increased complexity in navigation and hazard avoidance
Shift to creature-dependent platforming; crocodile seesaw mechanic requirement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ice-capped mountain top with frozen aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Environmental hazard escalation; movement difficulty increase

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Creepy dinosaur graveyard with Gothic visual tone; hostile atmosphere intensification
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss battle preparation; highest threat level; Ophelia rescue imminent

environmental_storytelling.technique: Themed world progression through five distinct biomes
environmental_storytelling.example: Journey from jungle through cave, water, ice to graveyard tells spatial rescue narrative; environments become increasingly hostile and thematically ominous
Five worlds each end with boss encounter, signaling progression gates and world completion states
Three to five levels per world allow narrative build-up within thematic zones before boss encounter
Layered background movement creates environmental immersion and temporal momentum through rescue journey (present in all versions except Atari ST)
Specific dinosaur and creature designs communicate zone identity: egg-popping dinosaurs in jungle, spiders in caves, crocodiles in water zones
Dinosaur graveyard rendered as creepy, separate environment signals it as hostile final destination; physical and visual separation from home

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Thrusts beer belly to attack, kicks while above ground, chucks rocks and boulders at enemies; picks up and throws projectiles
Uses rocks to shield from falling hazards; uses boulders as platforms for navigation
Pop out of eggs as encounter hazards; require rock throwing or physical attacks
Allow Chuck to consume them for sustenance; projectile targets that provide support
Serves as transport mount for traversal over obstacles; passive platform entity
Functions as seesaw mechanism to bounce Chuck over walls; designed as environmental puzzle solution
Knocks Ophelia unconscious and carries her to graveyard; final boss encounter; motivated by jealousy

notes: Chuck Rock operates on simple prehistoric theme with no explicit political factions—conflict is personal (jealous rival kidnaps wife) rather than ideological. World-state communication relies heavily on biome theming and enemy type variation rather than faction colors or symbols. The game uses slapstick humor (influenced by Tom and Jerry) and exaggerated character design (punk mohawk, loutish behavior) to communicate tone. Environmental storytelling is minimal; narrative advances through level progression and boss gates rather than world-state changes. Safety signals are primarily mechanical (rocks shield, creatures aid) rather than environmental markers. The Stone Age setting creates inherent danger-through-anachronism (dinosaurs and humans coexist) but this is world-texture rather than dynamic state communication.
